title: Creator Policy: Misleading Price Claims
text: Creators must not present false discounts, inflated list prices, or misleading before/after pricing. Claims like "70% off" must be supported by a real historical reference price. Violations include fake countdown timers, inconsistent pricing across surfaces, and doctored receipts. Enforcement actions range from content removal to creator account restrictions.

title: Content Policy: Counterfeit and Trademark Infringement
text: Listings or content that promote counterfeit goods, unauthorized replicas, or trademark misuse are prohibited. Signals include suspicious brand spelling variants, missing authenticity proof, and inconsistent packaging. Moderation should combine text cues, visual logo matching, seller history, and user reports. Repeat violations can trigger seller takedown and referral to manual review.

title: E-commerce Content Understanding: Product Attribute Extraction
text: To improve retrieval and moderation, extract structured attributes from multimodal content (title, description, images). Common attributes include brand, category, color, size, material, and functional claims. A model can be trained on labeled spans and image regions, then used to build attribute-level indexes and detect mismatches (e.g., "leather" in text but synthetic texture in images).

title: Moderation Workflow: Escalation and Appeal
text: When content is flagged, the system should store: triggering evidence, model scores, and a short explanation. For high-impact actions (creator suspension), require secondary review. Provide an appeal channel where creators can submit context and proof. Appeals should be decided within a defined SLA and logged for model training.

title: Spam Policy: Engagement Bait and Repetitive Content
text: Engagement bait ("like and follow" spam), repeated templates, and near-duplicate posts reduce ecosystem quality. Detection signals include high textual similarity, repeated audio/text overlays, and unnatural engagement patterns. Enforcement can include downranking, limited distribution, or removal for repeated offenders.

title: RAG for Policy QA: Authority and Recency
text: Policy QA systems must handle recency: later policy updates override earlier docs. Retrieval should prefer currently-active policy versions. A simple approach is two-stage retrieval: retrieve the relevant policy family (anchor), then resolve the latest effective version using metadata (effective date, supersedes links).

title: Data Quality: Deduplication and Label Noise
text: Large-scale training data often contains duplicates and noisy labels. Near-duplicate detection can use embeddings + clustering; label noise can be reduced via disagreement filtering, human-in-the-loop relabeling, and bootstrapping. For moderation datasets, ensure coverage of edge cases and avoid over-representing easy negatives.

title: Multimodal Similarity for Violations
text: To detect repeated violations, build multimodal embeddings for videos and product images, then cluster for similarity search. Use temporal sampling for videos, combine CLIP-style image embeddings with text embeddings, and index in an ANN store. For enforcement, surface the nearest neighbors as evidence for reviewers.
